Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The topic "Environmental Politics and International Relations" could cover international cooperation on transboundary water resources management (Target 6.5).

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The topic "Environmental Politics and International Relations" likely discusses international cooperation on energy, relevant to clean energy technology and investment (Target 7.a).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Topics like "International Economic Cooperation and Conflict" and "Multinational Corporations" directly relate to global economic systems, trade, and financial institutions (Targets 8.1, 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The topic "Multinational Corporations and International Relations" may tangentially touch on international infrastructure investment and technology transfer (Target 9.a).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course examines "International Organizations" and "International Economic Cooperation," directly addressing inequality between countries and representation in global institutions (Target 10.6).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: "Environmental Politics and International Relations" could address international frameworks for the sustainable management and efficient use of natural resources (Target 12.2).

Climate Action.reason: The topic "Environmental Politics and International Relations" explicitly addresses international efforts to integrate climate change measures into policies and planning (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: "International Law" and "Environmental Politics" likely cover legal frameworks like UNCLOS for the conservation and sustainable use of oceans (Target 14.c).

Life on Land.reason: "Environmental Politics and International Relations" would likely cover international agreements on conservation and sustainable use of terrestrial ecosystems (Target 15.1).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about international security, law, and organizations, directly supporting effective global institutions and promoting the international rule of law (Targets 16.3, 16.6, 16.8).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course focuses on "International Organizations," "International Law," and "International Economic Cooperation," which are the primary mechanisms for global partnerships and policy coherence (Targets 17.14, 17.16).
